About Shivoham Energies & Solutions

Your Trusted Rooftop Solar Partner in Maharashtra

Shivoham Energies & Solutions is a leading solar installation company headquartered in Nanded, Maharashtra. Over the past 5+ years, we have successfully commissioned 800+ residential, commercial, and government solar sites across the region.

Our team of certified solar experts handles every aspect — from roof shadow analysis and system design to installation, net metering, and post-installation service — so you get clean energy without any stress.

We make sure every customer gets the government subsidy they're entitled to, paired with the highest quality installation standards available in Maharashtra.

MNRE Approved Certified Experts ALMM-Listed Panels BIS Certified Equipment 800+ Sites Delivered

Have Questions?

Frequently Asked Questions

Everything you need to know about rooftop solar from Shivoham Energies & Solutions.

What is a rooftop solar system?
A rooftop solar system uses solar panels installed on your roof to generate electricity from sunlight using photovoltaic (PV) technology — for homes, businesses, and industries across Maharashtra.
How does rooftop solar work?
Solar panels absorb sunlight and convert it into DC electricity. A solar inverter then converts DC into AC electricity used by your appliances. Excess electricity is exported to the grid through net metering, earning you bill credits.
What are the main components of a solar system?
A complete rooftop solar system includes: Solar Panels, Solar Inverter, Mounting Structure, DC & AC Cables, Earthing System, Lightning Arrestor, Net Meter, and a Monitoring System. Shivoham Energies & Solutions supplies and installs all components.
Can solar panels work during cloudy or rainy days?
Yes! Solar panels continue generating electricity even in cloudy weather — generation is lower than on bright sunny days, but the system keeps producing. Maharashtra's high average sunshine ensures excellent annual output.
How long do solar panels last?
Most solar panels carry a 25-year performance warranty and a 10–12 year product warranty. With minimal maintenance, they routinely perform for 25–30 years.
Is rooftop solar safe?
Absolutely. Shivoham Energies & Solutions installs every system with proper earthing, lightning protection, quality wiring, and certified components — making rooftop solar completely safe for your home or business.
How much roof space do I need?
Approximately 100 sq. ft. of shadow-free roof area is needed for every 1 kW. A typical 3 kW home system needs just 300 sq. ft. — suitable for most urban homes in Maharashtra.
How much can I save on my electricity bill?
Most Shivoham residential customers save 70–90% on their electricity bills. Exact savings depend on your consumption and system size — contact us for a free personalised estimate.
Will solar panels damage my roof?
No. Properly installed solar mounting structures actually protect your roof from UV and rain. Our teams use industry-grade fixings with zero structural damage.
What happens during a power cut?
Standard on-grid systems automatically shut down during outages for safety. If you want backup power, ask Shivoham about our hybrid solar systems with battery storage — ideal for areas with frequent cuts.
How long does installation take?
Most residential installations by Shivoham Energies & Solutions are completed within 2–5 working days after all permits and approvals are in place.
Can solar run my AC, refrigerator, and motors?
Yes — if the system is correctly sized. Our team conducts a detailed energy audit before recommending capacity, ensuring your solar plant comfortably handles all your appliances.
Is rooftop solar suitable for commercial buildings?
Absolutely. Factories, offices, warehouses, hospitals, and hotels with high daytime consumption benefit the most. Shivoham Energies & Solutions specialises in high-capacity commercial rooftop systems.
What is the payback period for commercial solar?
Commercial systems typically recover their investment in 2–4 years — one of the best capital expenditures for any business. After payback, electricity is virtually free for 20+ years.
Can a commercial system be expanded later?
Yes. Shivoham designs modular systems that can be scaled as your energy needs or available rooftop area grows. Future expansion is factored into the initial design.
What is net metering and how does it benefit my business?
Net metering lets you export excess solar electricity to the grid and receive credits on your utility bill — meaning you never waste a unit generated and maximise your system's ROI.
Do you handle approvals for commercial projects?
Yes. Shivoham Energies & Solutions manages everything — site survey, system design, DISCOM approvals, net metering applications, and commissioning. You don't touch any paperwork.
How much government subsidy can I get?
Under PM Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana: 1 kW → ₹30,000 | 2 kW → ₹60,000 | 3 kW & above → up to ₹78,000. The subsidy is transferred directly to your bank account after installation.
Does Shivoham help with the subsidy application?
Yes — completely free of charge. Shivoham Energies & Solutions handles your PM Surya Ghar registration, documentation, DISCOM liaison, and net meter application from start to finish.
How much does a rooftop solar system cost?
Indicative costs: 1 kW ₹55,000–₹75,000 | 3 kW ₹1.5–₹2 lakh | 5 kW ₹2.5–₹3.5 lakh. After subsidy, your effective cost is significantly lower. Contact us for a free customised quote.
Can I get a bank loan or EMI for solar?
Yes. Many nationalised and private banks offer solar loans at attractive interest rates. We can help structure an EMI plan where your monthly savings often exceed the EMI amount.
What is the payback period for residential solar?
Residential systems typically pay back in 3–5 years — and with the government subsidy, even faster. After payback you enjoy 20+ years of near-free electricity with lifetime savings of 5–10x the investment.
Does solar increase my property value?
Yes. Properties with rooftop solar command a premium due to lower running costs and sustainability credentials — it's an investment that keeps paying you back.
Which type of solar panels does Shivoham Energies use?
We use high-efficiency Mono PERC and TopCon panels from ALMM-listed, MNRE-approved brands — delivering 18–23% efficiency and superior performance in low-light and high-temperature conditions.
What is shadow analysis?
Shadow analysis identifies shading from trees, tanks, or neighbouring buildings that could reduce generation. Shivoham performs a detailed on-site shadow study before designing your system to ensure maximum output.
What tilt angle is used in Maharashtra?
Panels are typically installed at 15°–25° tilt depending on latitude and roof type. Our engineers optimise the angle for maximum annual generation at your specific location.
What is the difference between on-grid, off-grid, and hybrid systems?
On-grid: grid-connected, uses net metering, lowest cost, no backup during outages. Off-grid: independent, uses batteries, higher cost. Hybrid: solar + battery + grid — works even during power cuts. Best system for your needs depends on your usage pattern.
What certifications should I look for?
Look for MNRE Approval, ALMM-listed panels, BIS Certification, and IEC Standards. All components supplied by Shivoham Energies & Solutions carry these certifications, ensuring quality and subsidy eligibility.
What maintenance is needed?
Solar is very low maintenance — mainly periodic panel cleaning, inverter checks, and an annual inspection. Shivoham Energies & Solutions offers scheduled maintenance visits to keep your system at peak efficiency.
